answer:
**"Ehd-e-Tifli" (The Age of Childhood)** is a poignant poem by Allama Iqbal from his collection *Bang-E-Dara Part 1* (1924), evoking nostalgic memories of infancy.[^1]

Here's the English translation:

> [p-4e88ba] **The Age of Childhood**  
> 
> [p-658473] The earth and sky, a newfound realm for me,  
> My mother's lap, a universe for me,  
> Each stir of life, a sign of glee for me,  
> My own tongue, just a wordless sound for me.  
> 
> [p-57fcd7] Though childhood's pain at times would make me weep,  
> The door-chain's rattling brought a joy so deep.  
> 
> [p-f7d6c6] Ah, how I'd stare for hours at the moon,  
> And watch it travel on its silent shoon,  
> 
> [p-09d3e2] And ask about its hills and deserts bare,  
> Then wonder at a fiction born of care.  
> 
> [p-9eb06f] My eye to see, my lips to speak were wrought;  
> It was no heart I had, but pure, inquiring thought.[^2]

The original Urdu and Roman transliteration are also available in the corpus if you'd like to see them.[^3]

[^1]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, Chapter 19: "Ehd-e-Tifli" ([translation](https://falsafa-corpus.org/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/19-ehd-e-tifli/translation/)).

[^2]: Ibid., [full chapter paragraphs](https://falsafa-corpus.org/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/19-ehd-e-tifli/translation/).

[^3]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, Chapter 19: "Ehd-e-Tifli" ([original](https://falsafa-corpus.org/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/19-ehd-e-tifli/original/)) and ([transliteration](https://falsafa-corpus.org/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/19-ehd-e-tifli/transliteration/)).
